brief_summary: Four-axis comparison ladder for aluminium, polycarbonate, and ABS case materials. Rows are weight (65% typical mass), case flex (under typing load), cost (relative manufacturing cost), and acoustic brightness. The ochre accent marks the leading material per axis — aluminium for weight and acoustic damping, PC for flex, ABS for cost. Makes explicit that no single material wins all four.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/case-materials-compared.mdx § 'Aluminium' — 'A CNC-machined aluminium 65% typically lands around 1.2 kg without PCB or switches'
apps/web/src/content/articles/case-materials-compared.mdx § 'Polycarbonate' — 'Less dense than aluminium so more acoustic energy escapes rather than being absorbed'
apps/web/src/content/articles/case-materials-compared.mdx § 'ABS' — 'Cheaper to produce than PC and far cheaper than machined aluminium'
